item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i literally cannot understand how this would work better as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the video in that first link has gone private

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (shouldn’t this be posted as an edit to the parent post?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great tale, bad SCP. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the few SCPs to actually freak me out. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't read through it. I had to skim, so, -1, sorry.
In fairness, the reason is because I knew you were going for some sort of mind screw the minute you started the format screw, and expecting it, the mundane read through the forest was too long.
Then you throw a bunch of crosslinks at me without much context at all. Maybe this is all a brilliant tie together I'm not getting, but until I do, and then come back to this, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would quote several other comments here, but I would spend too much lines to obtain no meaningful result, so I will be quick.

I really liked the idea and how it was done, in particular since what I thought was kinda confirmed by LordStonefish here in the comments, but I still don't feel like I can 100% +1 the article.

While the format screw is good I don't feel that this was the best way to write this idea. A bit more of…substance? Yeah, that's it. It doesn't give me any particular effect. It's cool, but I can't find anything really over-the-top to justify such a screw without maybe some log commenting on it after or similar notes. And I shouldn't need comments in the discussion to maybe guess what it is about.

Said so, I still +1. Because people can't really downvote such a work because "MUH TOO UNPROFESSIONAL", "MUH IT'S ON YOUTUBE".
I would leave it at 0, but since people here ain't capable of appreciating things properly, I must +1 to balance things out.

Still, a closing question: why the fuck "mouth-entity" with an eye under it? Good ol' horror or something that I missed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a nitpick, the "highly advanced (whatever they are)" implications are overdone, "quantum mechanics homework" bla bla bla… i think you should go for something "simpler" but more obscure… i know "non-euclidean geometry" is already stale in here, but maybe if you mentioned specifically what it is, i reckon it'd work better than "wowzers the things are so much more advanced than us"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started off slow, but holy shit, this is fucking great

item.title: I know, necroposting, but
item.content:encoded:
Weird. I like it, but a lot of the Command lines feel unrealistic and highly unprofessional.

I feel like that's the point

The "Flagged" things are kindof okay, but you'd need more of them to cover all of the memetic things as well as a disclaimer after the point it becomes clear Command has been infected with a memetic effect.

I disagree, I actually feel like there are too many of them. Also I think it's decently clear that Command gets infected early on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 because this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weird. I like it, but a lot of the Command lines feel unrealistic and highly unprofessional. The "Flagged" things are kindof okay, but you'd need more of them to cover all of the memetic things as well as a disclaimer after the point it becomes clear Command has been infected with a memetic effect. It would also help immensely if you had a designation for the mouth entities. Of course, there might be things i've missed, but i hope this helps.

-1 for now until it becomes more in the format the Foundation would use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this on my phone, so didn't get the audio autoplaying, sadly. In fact, I didn't even hear any audio at all, so I will probably have to reread this on my comp later.

Though I'm really not a fan om jumpscares, so I get the feeling that I mighn't like it.

I didn't get this one at all, really. Maybe it's aspies (aye, I have that), or maybe I'm just not good with these kinds of abstractions. Subtlety has never been my forte. Nonetheless, I was left wondering what this one was about, other than the mind-affecting extradimensional space anomaly. I suppose it just really didn't do it for me.

I'm also left wondering where the Daevite material in this is, because I couldn't find anything of the sort. Same with the 4000 contest thing, which I only got when you explained it here, and I still don't see it. That part, I suppose, is way too abstract for me to call it historical.

Might as well mention that the video didn't work for me either. I was hoping this would be something along the lines of an employee or researcher explaining something, but it was some random short film project? I suppose I see the connection to playing horror films in the Procedures section, but it felt kind of… I dunno, weak? As in, didn't quite manage to deliver the intended effect, and the departure from the anomaly in question served to be rather immersion-breaking for me.

In the end, it doesn't make much sense to me, and also doesn't really look like something put out by a rigorous scientific organization (which the Foundation is in my mind), e.g. some of the extraneous or inexplicable stuff at the end of the last exploration log would likely have been either redacted or commented upon, maybe even explained. So for me, this is a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of my what. Hard +1.

But, there was sound? I assume of the (rarely encountered these days) autoplaying background type? I didn't see any players anywhere, skipped the supposed memetic-incapacitator video, and my laptop is effectively on mute thanks to the speaker amplifier having bought the farm some months ago (putting earbuds in and out when you have no need to is a total pain). Probably a nice added bit of creepfactor but there's a lot of us who won't get it. Also I hope it's properly compressed for page bandwidth sakes ;)

Think there's still a typo in the first paragraph, by the way, unless it's deliberate medium-fuckery: "in a darkened room formin an hour"

item.title: Felicidades... creo
item.content:encoded:
Hey. I gave a +1 weeks before but now I want to say that this left me astonished. Today, after of doing a bit of LSD, smoking weed and watching Evangelion and Twin Peaks altogether, I translated this to Spanish.

Remember, now your scps can mindfuck all the world with TRANSLATION POWER!!!!!. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apology accepted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does, but it's not immediately obvious. unless you click on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't your Youtube channel have your real name as well, or am I mistaken in some way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still sarcasm is not the best way to get me to improve nor appreciate their point.

You're right, I'm sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, that's a good point. Still sarcasm is not the best way to get me to improve nor appreciate their point. You've convinced me that there's valid criticism in that statement but the tone and limited viewpoint (dunno if that's the right way of putting it), as well as incoherence, are still irksome and unhelpful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I totally get how it comes across as irritating when pointing it out is phrased in a short and snarky way, but that's still a vote based upon article content (the video and its host site are part of the content) and it's a legitimate criticism to say that it breaks immersion to have YouTube involved, even though that aspect is due to technical limitations.

Edit: Word, that's fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though wouldn't you agree commenting on something that arguably doesn't really affect the article in any way and outside the author's control is unhelpful to the situation?

It's something if the YouTube interface is breaking the site, but it's not the case. It's not like the video is monetized either. It doesn't change the experience whatsoever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To play devil's advocate, there are a lot of authors who get irritated when people don't explain why they downvoted. It's kind of a lose/lose situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve never seen Cabin In The Woods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was literally only the option I had. Wikidot only allows embedded videos of 50 MB (guaranteeing a silent film of two minutes), and Vimeo (which has been used before) destroys my privacy due to the fact my Vimeo channel has my real name in it and breaks immersion further.

It’s supremely irritating how many people have commented their downvote on this due to video and autoplaying audio, both of which have been used before in articles mind you, not out of dislike of the story or content (legitimate reasons to comment) but instead because it reminds them of another internet thing. It’s not going to make me change my mind and remove those elements, so why tell me and the world? You’re being irritating. Downvote and move on, get off your computer, and go read a book outside. Your opinion on my technical “gimmick” does not matter.

If you hated the story, by all means comment though. This is a literary website after all (of which film and music are a part).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote for video being on YouTube. It's really nice to known that the Foundation is funded by Netflix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a SCP-4000 contest entry, so the number likely hasn't been swapped yet. There are over 70 entries that need to be migrated over!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It says SCP-4000 in a few places and I am pretty sure that was unintentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The descriptions are evocative but I don't see a real substance here. It seems that it's captivating by being unintelligible.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't really say I enjoyed this one a ton.

The object itself, an extradimensional space in need of exploring is not particularly groundbreaking a concept, and by its nature usually requires a pretty solid exploration log to pull off, in my experience. And that's where things go a bit awry for this skip. While the descriptions here are passable as far as writing goes, the inhabitants of your little world from the sounds of it are basically different versions of the Sugarplum Fairy from Cabin in the Woods. In addition, the breakdown of the person at the helm of Command with such comments as:

Since I've joined The Foundation, I've sent a million D-Class like you to your deaths. Told them it would be alright. But why? Why you? Why this town, why this anomaly? I feel so guilty, so ashamed. My eyes hurt, I can't ever atone. You're not even dead. I can't ever atone. I am stuck with my sins for eternity. I am unclean, I am unclean, I am unclean, D-112.

and

What if it's not the Foundation, what if I'm the monster? Oh god, D-112. Oh god. It's all my fault. It's my fault.

are just really hard for me to take seriously, and to be frank, kill the immersion of your exploration more than had you had the command desperately trying to get ahold of the D-class the whole time.

Finally, the youtube video I felt was particularly gimmicky and didn't add a whole lot to the piece.

You certainly have an audience for this article, and maybe I'm just too much a square to appreciate some deeper logic or meaning going on, but as is the whole thing falls a little flat.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not at all. To be clear, my personal nitpicks don't get in the way of admiration for your style!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually the video was from a camera test for another idea that I happened to like, and therefore the actor did not give a full 100% to performance. I'm remaking it within the next week to fit the article more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But it hasn't disappeared up it's own ass, yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so torn on this one, as I was with your 3999. The atmosphere here is absolutely top notch, and I LOVE the surreal breaks in the log - and the way some are more subtle than others - that at best make the reader second guess themself. And the Danielewski and James Joyce influences are huge, and I love me some Danielewski. It's just that, to me, this is a little closer to "Only Revolutions" than "House of Leaves", where the writing gets dangerously close to disappearing up its own ass. I really hope that doesn't sound rude; I don't intend that. I love your style and the madness and horror that often feels right on my peripheral vision as I read it. I guess my personal taste just prefers that it creeps in rather than gets waved in my face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is too creative not to upvote.

+ show block
– hide block

The terror of all our aspirations being neatly fit into a predictable mold to the wailing commentary of someone who is so sorry for this result, the central figure only needing real check-ins of represented change every decade or so, is horrifying. The juxtaposing of the ground-based mundane and daily motions with the spaghettification into the deepest reaches of space and sanity is just so close-to-home, and so real despite being so unreal.

Having said that, I saw at one problematic sentence in the way of typos. I’ll reproduce it here instead of just changing them via a revision:

Personnel finishing shifts are to be blindfold and led to lie down in a darkened room within in an hour under the supervision a

Should that be “blindfolded”? “Led to lie down” is a really poetic phrase but i’m not sure it’s working as it would in more floral settings. “within in an hour” doesn’t seem right grammatically maybe i’m misreading it. A bit confusing either way.

The article also features some inconsistencies with the placement of footnotes with respect to sentence-ending punctuation.

Having pointed those out, I’m not sure if these are supposed to be there, playing a part in the heavily indicated memetic corruption, but I’d hope they were not intentional because that use doesn’t pronounce itself well enough.

I’d be quick to accept that this is over my head, but I don’t get the connection between the anomaly and the memetic corruption in the article itself.

My biggest issue is the video, which does nothing for me and imo severely handicaps the article as something unbidden and awkward to watch. I commend you for your audacity making it and including it in the format but it seems really gratuitous to imply such an indulgence is serving an anti-memetic purpose for the Foundation’s official documentation.

The video creates so many criticisms that don’t need to be part of an article, such as the casting and delivery of the line, which are both unconvincing. This is not your fault and unavoidable but Youtube’s video suggestions after the completion of the video is immersion-breaking, even though the presence of YouTube is integrated into the documentation.

The autoplaying audio, however, is brilliant and a great idea.

The two climb into the bed and proceed to copulate…The previous bedtime routine repeats.

You must not be married ;P

Why does the Foundation-authored narration of the video feed become so incoherent at the end? Given an explanation, why doesn’t the Foundation remove the seemingly meaningless additions in the way they remove superfluous chatter in many audio/video logs?

Anyway, you seem to be the premier surrealist of the site and your work never fails to put me in a state of comfortable dissociation from my environment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1 for autoplaying audio. This isn't myspace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a heads up, most speakers- even very high quality ones- can't produce infrasound, so that drone you had in the music isn't really going to work. This is especially true of the laptop and cell phone speakers that readers would be listening to this through. I really cranked up the volume on my speakers and was only able to get a "helicopter noise" effect from the overtones, which while still eerie, isn't the infamous infrasound effect.

Aside from that incredibly minor nitpick, I really enjoyed this.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of SCP-3999 in that it's almost like poetry. I'm still wrapping my head around this one, but all I know is that I enjoyed it. More great surrealist work from you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I ask what happens in the video? I'm too scared to watch it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip is one of the most interesting I've had the pleasure of proof reading. From the log to the file itself, it is perfectly crafted to be interesting as shit. Although some people didn't get a good grasp on it, many more did and are thankful for the work and effort you put into this wonderful skip!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I let it sit for a bit, and I changed my mind. I still think the article is a bit rough around the edges, but damn if it doesn’t hit me in exactly the right spots. I don’t remember being this heavily affected by an SCP since 3999, and as I said, that’s no easy feat… so yeah, take my upvote and my existential fears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agh, I’m really conflicted on this one… on one hand, the visceral reaction this article drew from me was almost comparable to that of 3999, and that’s no easy feat… but on the other hand, the writing itself felt stilted at times (I feel that the word salad descriptions worked a lot better in 3999 than they did here, for example), and most importantly, I’m worried that I didn’t really “get” it. I’m pretty sure I understand the fears that the article is drawing on and the themes that it’s tackling (the horror of a mundane, ordinary life, one’s insignificance in comparison to the cosmos, said insignificance suddenly slamming into you whenever you stop to look up at the stars, etc.), but even after doing some research (AKA googling the title and trying to understand the Calabi-Yau manifold article), I still can’t help but worry that a lot of it went over my head. Given the article’s inspirations, maybe this sense of confusion was intentional, but either way, it’s still enough to make me hold off on upvoting for now. Maybe after letting it sit for a little bit, I’ll change my mind.

Side note: I almost want to write an SCP-Declassified article on this, given the “research” I did and the fact that I feel it deserves a lot of recognition… but, well, an SCP-Declassified kinda requires the author to have actually fully understood the SCP, so I doubt that’d go well…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like it. I feel like I like it. But…I also feel lost and confused and don't really grasp it. It's one of those "I can't upvote, and I can't exactly tell you why not, other than I don't want to" sort of things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What in fuck?

Oh it's Lord Stonefish.

Well good work I guess, I may just be a slow reader but the audio cut out before the mentions of humming even popped up in the exploration log. Not sure if that's intentional. MY only complaint is that I feel like the early third section with the mouth entity nurses and regular humans in the garden doesn't really fit in with the overall narrative you have set up, but I may be missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I'm sure there's some internal logic here, but it's both too initially difficult and not really interesting enough to puzzle out, with the end result just looking like kind of a mess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's both the history of an average person's life, the history of the universe, and the history of the pre-universe (pre Big-Bang, pre "In the beginning…") Fundamentally, SCP-4000 is a place, a way-station, that connects these three.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure it's the history of the universe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please pardon my confusion. I thought the 4000 entries were to reference history in some way, and I can't identify the history hook here. Would someone kindly correct my lack of understanding?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once again, you have broken the traditional format and produced an amazing article. I don't like this as much as 3999, but it's definitely up there. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont understand this at all but it made me feel immersed so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, a sentence was missing from the description about the memetics. It was in earlier drafts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

So this is an alternate earth that's Not Quite Right, spatially or otherwise, and once you walk into it, you get placed into a mindless dreamlike timeline of an anomalous facsimile of 'average middle-class life'?

And the historical theme connection is… the way it looks at standard middle-class life? I'm super unclear on what this has to do with history.

I don't know about this. It's very high-effort, clearly, but I found the video really underwhelming and unnecessary.

All access to the unredacted transcripts of expeditions into SCP-4000 are to be protected by Bergman/Lynch incapacitation memetic videos. The Department of Memetics has analyzed memetic corruption within the logs for SCP-4000 and decided that the footage in the Bergman/Lynch memes should be sourced from horror films and uploaded to a private Youtube channel for maximum effectiveness.

Why? There are no details given on the memetic effect at hand with this anomaly. It's not even mentioned in the Description that memetics are involved.

I didn't like that all of Command's unprofessional comments were just This statement has been flagged for potential memetic corruption'd away when there are no details on a memetic effect even being involved in the Description. Clearly the implication is supposed to be that the log is the first memetic hazard event(s) they encountered, but I feel like the Description is almost a different anomaly than what happens in the log because the log has all of the details and the Description has barely any.

And "mouth-entities" drives me bonkers. It's used so many times and it reads so awkwardly. I see no reason that it can't be SCP-4000-2. "SCP-4000-2 instances inhabit SCP-4000." If the reason is 'memetic influence' then sure, cool, but we still don't know what that influence actually is. There's not even a paragraph.

That's my issue with this, I think. This article bothers me because it's not written like an SCP article, and there's not enough of a meaningful format screw to make up for that like there was with your entry last year. If I were criticizing the in-universe writer, I would say "why didn't you take all of those key elements of the anomaly that are messily revealed in the exploration and put them in the Description? That's what this format is for."

Given others' responses, I'm almost wondering if I'm missing something major here.

Edit: I was going to downvote because of the memetics stuff, but since that's fixed, novote. I don't particularly like this for the other reasons given (and seconding rume and MMG above), but I can respect the value of the narrative in that log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling this. The middle portions of the log are evocative and well written, but the beginning and end of the scip just feel tacked on, like a standard set-up and mind screw/word salad ending that could be used for any number of possible articles but don't add anything or lead to a coherent or interesting summation on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I don't Understand it, but i like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i made a shitpost out of it already lmao (serious actual art will come later when i come up w/ an idea on how to draw this good and cool)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I just looked at the source and it looks like you have two http://'s. I assume that's unintentional. Also assuming it's the humming sound 112 heard. :B (Imma go listen to it now.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both in the video, and in the audio. If you read too quickly, you might miss the last one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On reflection, I didn't notice any jumpscares, which I tend to be sensitive to, nor did I hear any sound? Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All fixed.
